message: fix: recompute repl backlog window on CONFIG SET (frankenredis-ceck)

files.patch:
@@ -104,6 +104,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenredis-bqot","title":"Track real total_reads_processed and total_writes_processed in INFO stats","description":"# Track real total_reads_processed and total_writes_processed in INFO stats

## Why
`INFO stats` still hardcodes `total_reads_processed:0` and `total_writes_processed:0` even though command metadata already distinguishes read and write surfaces.

## Scope
- Add store-owned counters for client-visible read and write commands.
- Use command flags from `fr_command::get_command_flags` to classify completed commands in runtime.
- Wire `INFO stats` to emit the real counters.
- Reset them in `CONFIG RESETSTAT`.
- Add focused tests for one readonly command and one write command, plus INFO visibility.

## Files
- `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs`

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-command info_stats_reports_expire_and_evict_counters -- --exact --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-runtime --lib total_reads_and_writes_processed_counts_classified_commands -- --exact --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p fr-runtime --lib`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T00:00:19.374029335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T00:02:25.729165806Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T00:02:25.728889032Z","close_reason":"Implemented store-backed read/write counters, INFO stats wiring, resetstat coverage, and focused tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-bxlo","title":"Build redis-benchmark-compatible TCP load generator with HdrHistogram latency recording","description":"# Benchmark Load Generator

## What
A Rust binary that connects to a running FrankenRedis (or Redis) instance via TCP, sends configurable workloads, and records precise latency histograms.

## Why
The project claims "profile-proven performance" but has zero throughput or latency measurements. This tool is the foundation for ALL performance claims.

## Implementation
1. New binary: `crates/fr-server/src/bin/fr_benchmark.rs` (or dedicated crate `crates/fr-bench/`)
2. Use `std::net::TcpStream` (blocking OK for benchmark client)
3. Workloads to implement:
   - SET: random keys, configurable value size (default 3 bytes like redis-benchmark)
   - GET: random existing keys
   - INCR: atomic counter
   - LPUSH/LPOP: list operations
   - HSET/HGET: hash operations
   - Mixed: configurable ratio of reads/writes
4. Parameters: `--host`, `--port`, `--clients` (concurrent connections), `--requests` (total), `--pipeline` (commands per batch), `--keyspace` (number of unique keys), `--datasize`
5. Latency recording: use `hdrhistogram` crate for O(1) percentile queries
6. Output: JSON with ops/sec, p50/p95/p99/p999 latency in microseconds, total time, bytes transferred
7. Human-readable summary to stdout

## Validation
- Run against both frankenredis and legacy redis on same machine
- Verify ops/sec is within 2x of legacy redis for basic SET/GET
- Verify latency histogram is populated and percentiles are sane

## Files
- crates/fr-bench/Cargo.toml (new crate)
- crates/fr-bench/src/main.rs
- Cargo.toml workspace members update","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:10:23.067263275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:27:03.242987153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:27:03.242608697Z","close_reason":"Implemented fr-bench TCP benchmark harness with histogram reporting and remote-verified validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-d16","title":"Implement persistent replica streaming, reconnect, and partial-resync semantics","description":"The server now performs a one-shot live REPLICAOF/PSYNC handshake and FULLRESYNC apply over TCP, but it does not yet maintain a long-lived replication socket, continuously ingest streamed writes, or reuse replid/offset for reconnect-driven CONTINUE partial resync. Implement persistent stream ownership, reconnect/backoff, and partial-resync state continuity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T16:16:24.588396131Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T22:49:41.588257105Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T22:49:41.587460488Z","close_reason":"Already implemented and verified in current tree","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
